text: Rule No. 1: Never lose money. Rule No. 2: Never forget Rule No. 1.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Be fearful when others are greedy, and greedy when others are fearful.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Price is what you pay. Value is what you get.
author: Warren Buffett

text: It's far better to buy a wonderful company at a fair price than a fair company at a wonderful price.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Our favorite holding period is forever.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Only buy something that you'd be perfectly happy to hold if the market shut down for 10 years.
author: Warren Buffett

text: The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Risk comes from not knowing what you're doing.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Never invest in a business you cannot understand.
author: Warren Buffett

text: The most important thing in investing is knowing your circle of competence and staying inside of it.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Wide diversification is only required when investors do not understand what they are doing.
author: Warren Buffett

text: It takes 20 years to build a reputation and five minutes to ruin it.
author: Warren Buffett

text: I try to buy stock in businesses that are so wonderful that an idiot can run them. Because sooner or later, one will.
author: Warren Buffett

text: The best thing that happens to us is when a great company gets into temporary trouble. We want to buy them when they're on the operating table.
author: Warren Buffett

text: In the business world, the rearview mirror is always clearer than the windshield.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Chains of habit are too light to be felt until they are too heavy to be broken.
author: Warren Buffett

text: The difference between successful people and really successful people is that really successful people say no to almost everything.
author: Warren Buffett

text: If you aren't willing to own a stock for ten years, don't even think about owning it for ten minutes.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Someone's sitting in the shade today because someone planted a tree a long time ago.
author: Warren Buffett

text: You only have to do a very few things right in your life so long as you don't do too many things wrong.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Invert, always invert.
author: Charlie Munger

text: All I want to know is where I'm going to die, so I'll never go there.
author: Charlie Munger

text: The big money is not in the buying and the selling, but in the waiting.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Spend each day trying to be a little wiser than you were when you woke up.
author: Charlie Munger

text: The desire to get rich fast is pretty dangerous.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Knowing what you don't know is more useful than being brilliant.
author: Charlie Munger

text: People calculate too much and think too little.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Take a simple idea and take it seriously.
author: Charlie Munger

text: The iron rule of nature is: you get what you reward for. If you want ants to come, you put sugar on the floor.
author: Charlie Munger

text: It is remarkable how much long-term advantage people like us have gotten by trying to be consistently not stupid, instead of trying to be very intelligent.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Three things ruin people: drugs, liquor, and leverage.
author: Charlie Munger

text: The world is not driven by greed; it's driven by envy.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Mimicking the herd invites regression to the mean.
author: Charlie Munger

text: If you buy things you do not need, soon you will have to sell things you need.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Acknowledge and remove your ignorance is the beginning of wisdom.
author: Charlie Munger

text: A great business at a fair price is superior to a fair business at a great price.
author: Charlie Munger

text: You don't have to be brilliant, only a little bit wiser than the other guys, on average, for a long, long time.
author: Charlie Munger

text: The best armor of old age is a well-spent life preceding it.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Show me the incentive and I'll show you the outcome.
author: Charlie Munger

text: Opportunity comes to the prepared mind.
author: Charlie Munger

text: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: The intelligent investor is a realist who sells to optimists and buys from pessimists.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: In the short run, the market is a voting machine but in the long run, it is a weighing machine.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: The margin of safety is always dependent on the price paid.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: The individual investor should act consistently as an investor and not as a speculator.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: Buy not on optimism, but on arithmetic.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: The investor's chief problem — and even his worst enemy — is likely to be himself.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: Have the courage of your knowledge and experience. If you have formed a conclusion from the facts and if you know your judgment is sound, act on it.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: To achieve satisfactory investment results is easier than most people realize; to achieve superior results is harder than it looks.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: The essence of investment management is the management of risks, not the management of returns.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: Know what you own, and know why you own it.
author: Peter Lynch

text: Go for a business that any idiot can run — because sooner or later, any idiot probably is going to run it.
author: Peter Lynch

text: The person that turns over the most rocks wins the game.
author: Peter Lynch

text: Far more money has been lost by investors preparing for corrections, or trying to anticipate corrections, than has been lost in corrections themselves.
author: Peter Lynch

text: Behind every stock is a company. Find out what it's doing.
author: Peter Lynch

text: If you spend more than 13 minutes analyzing economic and market forecasts, you've wasted 10 minutes.
author: Peter Lynch

text: Investing without research is like playing stud poker and never looking at the cards.
author: Peter Lynch

text: The key to making money in stocks is not to get scared out of them.
author: Peter Lynch

text: Time is on your side when you own shares of superior companies.
author: Peter Lynch

text: You have to know what you own and why you own it. 'This baby is a cinch to go up!' doesn't count.
author: Peter Lynch

text: The four most dangerous words in investing are: 'This time it's different.'
author: John Templeton

text: Bull markets are born on pessimism, grow on skepticism, mature on optimism, and die on euphoria.
author: John Templeton

text: The time of maximum pessimism is the best time to buy, and the time of maximum optimism is the best time to sell.
author: John Templeton

text: Diversify. In stocks and bonds, as in much else, there is safety in numbers.
author: John Templeton

text: If you want to have a better performance than the crowd, you must do things differently from the crowd.
author: John Templeton

text: The most important quality for an investor is temperament, not intellect.
author: Warren Buffett

text: We don't have to be smarter than the rest. We have to be more disciplined than the rest.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Returns decrease as motion increases.
author: Warren Buffett

text: The most important investment you can make is in yourself.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Margin of safety — the three most important words in investing.
author: Warren Buffett

text: The secret to investing is to figure out the value of something and then pay a lot less.
author: Joel Greenblatt
source: The Little Book That Beats the Market

text: Choosing individual stocks without any idea of what you're looking for is like running through a dynamite factory with a burning match.
author: Joel Greenblatt
source: You Can Be a Stock Market Genius

text: In investing, what is comfortable is rarely profitable.
author: Robert Arnott

text: The stock market is filled with individuals who know the price of everything, but the value of nothing.
author: Philip Fisher

text: I'd rather own a few stocks that I know a lot about than many stocks that I know little about.
author: Philip Fisher

text: The stock market is never obvious. It is designed to fool most of the people, most of the time.
author: Jesse Livermore

text: There is nothing new in Wall Street. There can't be because speculation is as old as the hills.
author: Jesse Livermore

text: Money is made by sitting, not trading.
author: Jesse Livermore

text: It never was my thinking that made the big money for me. It always was my sitting.
author: Jesse Livermore

text: The biggest risk of all is not taking one.
author: Mellody Hobson

text: Compound interest is the eighth wonder of the world. He who understands it, earns it; he who doesn't, pays it.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The goal of a successful trader is to make the best trades. Money is secondary.
author: Alexander Elder

text: Investing should be more like watching paint dry or watching grass grow. If you want excitement, take $800 and go to Las Vegas.
author: Paul Samuelson

text: Markets can remain irrational longer than you can remain solvent.
author: John Maynard Keynes

text: The four most expensive words in the English language are 'This time it's different.'
author: Carmen Reinhart

text: Good investing is boring.
author: George Soros

text: It's not whether you're right or wrong that's important, but how much money you make when you're right and how much you lose when you're wrong.
author: George Soros

text: I'm only rich because I know when I'm wrong.
author: George Soros

text: Do not be embarrassed by your failures, learn from them and start again.
author: Richard Branson

text: The individual investor should act consistently as an investor and not as a speculator.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: Successful investing is about managing risk, not avoiding it.
author: Benjamin Graham

text: I will tell you how to become rich. Close the doors. Be fearful when others are greedy. Be greedy when others are fearful.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Buy a stock the way you would buy a house. Understand and like it such that you'd be content to own it in the absence of any market.
author: Warren Buffett

text: Time in the market beats timing the market.
author: Ken Fisher

text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Don't look for the needle in the haystack. Just buy the haystack!
author: John Bogle
source: The Little Book of Common Sense Investing

text: If you have trouble imagining a 20% loss in the stock market, you shouldn't be in stocks.
author: John Bogle

text: The miracle of compounding returns is overwhelmed by the tyranny of compounding costs.
author: John Bogle
source: The Little Book of Common Sense Investing

text: Focus on what you can control: costs, diversification, time horizon, and your own behavior.
author: John Bogle

text: Do not save what is left after spending, but spend what is left after saving.
author: Warren Buffett
